# Getting help

Thanks for using VortexFlow! Here's where to go, depending on what you need.

## Questions & how-to

- **Usage questions, "how do I…", ideas, show-and-tell** → open a
  [GitHub Discussion](../../discussions). This keeps the issue tracker focused on
  actionable work and makes answers searchable for others.
- **Check the docs first** — `README.md` and the `docs/` directory cover setup,
  deployment, and architecture.

## Bug reports & feature requests

- **Reproducible bug** → open an [issue](../../issues/new/choose) using the bug
  template. Include your version/commit, deployment mode (Docker/local/agent),
  and steps to reproduce.
- **Feature request** → open an issue describing the problem you're trying to
  solve, not just the solution you have in mind.

## Security issues

**Do not** report vulnerabilities through public issues, discussions, or PRs.
Follow the private disclosure process in [`SECURITY.md`](../SECURITY.md).

## Contributing

Want to fix or build something yourself? See [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](../CONTRIBUTING.md).

> VortexFlow is community-supported open source. There is no SLA on free
> support, but maintainers and the community try to help as time allows.
